mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% and remains elevated for 2+ consecutive months, the restrictive monetary stance compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates. This triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as market participants price in sustained borrowing costs.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive rates above neutral
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0% for 2+ months, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) over 3 consecutive months signals labor market deterioration and rising recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations compress.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines month-over-month for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of recession-signal instances, or correlation between PAYEMS 3-month decline and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) exhibit month-over-month growth acceleration above 1.5% while simultaneously housing starts (HOUST) remain above 1400 thousand units, indicating robust consumer demand and housing activity. This joint strength predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through earnings growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM growth > 1.5% AND HOUST > 1400 thousand units
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 697557.0M and HOUST at 1452.0K as of 2023-07-15
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with sustained consumer and housing strength
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 45% of joint-trigger instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month for 2 consecutive months, signaling supply-side weakness and potential stagflation risk. This triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice earnings and growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines MoM for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of consecutive INDPRO decline instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period while nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) simultaneously declines, creating a dual labor market deterioration signal. This joint weakness predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through recession probability repricing.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months AND PAYEMS declines YoY
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.8% and PAYEMS at 156419.0K as of 2023-08-15
regime_hint: recession onset or labor market inflection point
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of dual-deterioration instances, or joint interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.20) in logistic regression predicting negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) declines month-over-month for 2 consecutive months while the federal funds rate remains above 5.0%, signaling disinflation success under restrictive policy. This combination predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as markets anticipate eventual rate cuts.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL declines MoM for 2 consecutive months AND FEDFUNDS > 5.0%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 306.269 and FEDFUNDS at 5.33% as of 2023-08-15
regime_hint: disinflation phase within monetary tightening cycle
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of disinflation-with-high-rates instances, or interaction coefficient between CPIAUCSL decline and FEDFUNDS level is not significantly positive (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarter-over-quarter remains positive and above 0.5% annualized rate, indicating sustained economic expansion. This always-on trend signal predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days through baseline earnings growth expectations.
regime_hint: any regime with positive GDP growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of positive-GDP-growth periods, or correlation between GDPC1 growth rate and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates to above 1.0% month-over-month while simultaneously the unemployment rate (UNRATE) remains below 4.0%, indicating robust supply expansion coupled with tight labor markets. This dual strength predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through productivity and earnings growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.4886 and UNRATE at 3.8% as of 2023-08-15
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with supply-side strength and tight labor market
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of joint-strength instances, or interaction coefficient between INDPRO growth and low UNRATE is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in multivariate regression.
